news.headline: Knowles (NYSE: KN) reports Q3 2024 financial results with revenues meeting expectations. Positive outlook for Q4.

Knowles specializes in providing capacitors, RF filters, advanced medtech microphones, and balanced armature speakers for various demanding applications worldwide. This announcement was made from the company's headquarters in Itasca, Illinois.

news.headline: Cambridge's tech industry growth highlights UK's income inequality issue.
L3Harris Technologies Q3 2024: Revenue at $5.3B, up 8%, EPS at $2.10, up 4%, Non-GAAP EPS at $3.34, up 5%. Book-to-bill ratio at 1.4x.
news.summary: L3Harris Technologies (NYSE: LHX) announced a positive financial performance in the third quarter of 2024, with a revenue of $5.3 billion, showing an 8% increase from the same period in 2023. The diluted EPS for the quarter stood at $2.10, marking a 4% increase from Q3 2023. Additionally, the non-GAAP diluted EPS for Q3 2024 reached $3.34, reflecting a 5% rise from the previous year. The company also reported a remarkable book-to-bill ratio of 1.4x. More information regarding non-GAAP results can be found in the tables starting from page 11.

news.headline: Skechers reports record-breaking quarterly sales of $2.35 billion in Q3 2024, with a 15.9% increase and diluted earnings per share up 35.5%.
news.summary: Skechers U.S.A., Inc. has reported financial results for the third quarter ended on September 30, 2024. The company, known as The Comfort Technology CompanyTM and a prominent global footwear leader, achieved record quarterly sales of $2.35 billion, marking a substantial 15.9% increase. Wholesale sales saw a growth of 20.6%, while Direct-to-Consumer sales increased by 9.6%. Diluted earnings per share also experienced a noteworthy surge, reaching $1.26, a 35.5% rise compared to the previous period. Additionally, Skechers repurchased $90.0 million of Class A common stock, showing confidence in their performance. The company expressed satisfaction with these results, indicating a strong performance in the market.

news.headline: TELA Bio closes $46M public offering of 14.67M common stock shares at $2.25/share, pre-funded warrants for 5.8M shares at $2.2499/share.
news.summary: TELA Bio, Inc., based in MALVERN, made an announcement on October 24, 2024, regarding the completion of its previously mentioned underwritten public offering. The offering consisted of 14,670,000 shares of common stock, with an inclusion of the underwriters’ full option to purchase additional shares. These shares were sold at a price of $2.25 per share to the public. In addition to common stock, pre-funded warrants were offered at a price of $2.2499 per warrant, giving investors the option to purchase 5,800,000 shares of common stock. The total gross proceeds from this offering amounted to $46 million prior to deducting underwriting discounts, commissions, and other estimated expenses, under the assumption that the pre-funded warrants are not exercised. It is important to note that all the shares of common stock and pre-funded warrants were sold by TELA.

news.headline: GSI Technology reported Q2 fiscal 2025 results: Net revenues $4.6 million, gross margin 38.6%, operating loss $(5.6) million. CEO notes potential in SRAM business turnaround. Cut costs for $3.5M savings. Q3 outlook: revenues $4.7M-$5.5M, gross margin 50%-52%. Cash at $18.4M. More on: www.gsitechnology.com.
news.summary: GSI Technology, Inc. reported financial results for the second fiscal quarter ended September 30, 2024. Net revenues for the quarter were $4.55 million, a decrease from $4.67 million in the previous quarter and $5.71 million in the same period last year. Gross margin was 38.6%, down from 46.3% in the previous quarter and 54.7% in the same period last year. The operating loss was $5.58 million, compared to an operating income of $1.08 million in the previous quarter. Net loss was $5.46 million, resulting in a loss per share of $0.21. The company highlighted a turnaround in their SRAM business, expecting increased orders from existing customers and significant cost-cutting measures to increase cash flow. The outlook for the third quarter includes projected revenues between $4.7 million and $5.5 million with a gross margin of 50% to 52%. At the end of the quarter, the company had $18.4 million in cash and cash equivalents. Total operating expenses were $7.3 million, and stockholders' equity was $33.3 million as of September 30, 2024. Management conducted a conference call to discuss the financial results and outlook for the company.

news.headline: A&B reported Q3 results: net income $19.0M, EPS $0.26, CRE operating profit $22.8M. FFO $28.2M, Adjusted FFO $23.4M. Leased occupancy at 94.0%. They revised 2024 guidance with Net Income per diluted share now at $0.74 to $0.82, FFO per diluted share at $1.27 to $1.35. Leasing spreads for the portfolio were 15.3%. Acquisition of an 81,500 sq ft facility. Land Operations operating profit at $7.9M. Balance sheet shows a total debt of $472.2M and equity market capitalization of $1.4B. New ATM equity offering program established. Liquidity stands at $445.9M. Subsequent to the quarter, an extension of the revolving credit facility was made with $450.0M of borrowing capacity. A dividend of $0.2225 per share was paid in Q3. Follows A&B's focus on Hawai'i commercial real estate assets.
news.summary: Alexandar & Baldwin, Inc., a Hawai'i-based real estate company, reported Q3 2024 net income of $19.0 million, or $0.26 per share, and CRE operating profit of $22.8 million. They also announced FFO of $28.2 million and Adjusted FFO of $23.4 million for the same period. Leased occupancy was 94.0%, with improved portfolio leasing spreads at 15.3%. They acquired an 81,500 sq ft facility and updated their credit facility. Financially, debt was $472.2 million, with a market cap of $1.9 billion. They revised their 2024 guidance to $0.74-$0.82 per diluted share for net income and $1.27-$1.35 for FFO per share. Land Operations saw an increase in operating profit to $7.9 million. The company had $445.9 million in total liquidity. Further details on their operations and financials are available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: SS&C Technologies Q3 2024 earnings: GAAP revenue $1.46B, up 7.3%, EPS $0.65, up 6.6%, Adjusted revenue $1.47B, up 7.3%, Adjusted EPS $1.29, up 10.3%. Record adjusted consolidated EBITDA $566.2M, up 6.0%. Operating cash flow $902.0M, 9.1% increase. Ended Q3 with $694.7M cash, $7,243.1M gross debt. Acquisition of Battea-Class Action Services for $670M. Guidance for Q4 2024: Adjusted revenue $1.46-1.50B, Adjusted EPS $1.29-1.35. FY 2024: Adjusted Revenue $5.815-5.855B, Adjusted Net Income $1.299-1.315B. Earnings call today.
news.summary: SS&C Technologies Holdings, Inc. reported its Q3 2024 financial results, with GAAP revenue at $1,465.8 million, up 7.3%, and fully diluted GAAP Earnings Per Share at $0.65, up 6.6%. Adjusted revenue was at $1,466.8 million, up 7.3%, and Adjusted Diluted Earnings Per Share at $1.29, up 10.3%. The company's operating cash flow for the nine months ended on Sep 30, 2024, showed a 9.1% increase, reaching $902.0 million. SS&C ended Q3 with $694.7 million in cash and cash equivalents and $7,243.1 million in gross debt. The company's Q3 2024 highlights included buying back 1.2 million shares for $89.4 million and reporting GAAP net income of $164.4 million, up 5.4%. The adjusted consolidated EBITDA for Q3 was $566.2 million, a 6.0% increase. SS&C also acquired Battea-Class Action Services for approximately $670 million. The Q4 2024 guidance includes adjusted revenue of $1,460.0 - $1,500.0 million and adjusted net income of $329.0 - $345.0 million. SS&C's earnings call for Q3 2024 took place on October 24, 2024. The company does not provide reconciliations of guidance for adjusted revenues and net income to comparable GAAP measures.

news.headline: Yardi Breeze wins 10 awards from verified users in G2 Fall 2024 Reports for property management software platform.

news.summary: In the third quarter of 2024, SS&C Technologies Holdings, Inc. reported a GAAP revenue of $1,465.8 million, marking a 7.3% increase. news.headline: SolarEdge Technologies praises US Treasury’s final rule on Advanced Manufacturing Credit, aiming to boost manufacturing across the US. Company, NASDAQ: SEDG, has 2 US facilities operational thanks to Section 45X, increasing production of inverters and power optimizers.
news.summary: SolarEdge Technologies, Inc., a leader in smart energy technology, has expressed its support for the U.S. Department of the Treasury's new rules for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credit. The Section 45X provision will aid in the company's expansion of manufacturing in the U.S. SolarEdge currently operates two facilities in the country where they produce inverters and power optimizers. This development marks a significant advancement for SolarEdge's residential and commercial product lines.

news.headline: Century Aluminum praises Biden Admin for finalizing rules enabling 45X credit for U.S. primary aluminum, certain raw materials.
news.summary: Specific raw materials have been deemed eligible for the Production Tax Credit concerning primary aluminum production in the United States. This move aims to support the domestic aluminum industry and encourage production by providing tax incentives. The tax credit is applicable for a range of raw materials used in the manufacturing process of primary aluminum, including [list of specific raw materials]. This initiative is significant for the industry as it could lead to an increase in domestic production capacity by [percentage or numeric value], creating jobs and boosting economic growth.

news.headline: Seelos Therapeutics postpones its 2024 Annual Meeting again, focused on CNS disorders. (Largest info: Seelos Therapeutics, OTCQB: SEEL)
news.summary: Recently, on October 24, 2024, Seelos Therapeutics, Inc. (OTCQB: SEEL) disclosed details regarding their 2024 Annual Meeting. Seelos, a notable clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company concentrating on crafting treatments for central nervous system disorders and rare diseases, shared this news.
